channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Brantford researchers use VR exergames to support fitness and cognitive engagement in older adults with dementia
item.description: Researchers at Wilfrid Laurier University in Brantford, Ontario, are exploring innovative ways to help older adults with dementia remain physically and cognitively active through virtual reality (VR) ...

item.title: Metro Manila commuters experience heavy traffic and overcrowded PUVs on first workday after Holy Week
item.description: Many commuters in Metro Manila encountered significant challenges as they returned to work on Monday following the Holy Week break. Long queues formed early in the morning, particularly at the PHILCOA...

item.title: Palace Confirms President Marcos is in Good Health
item.description: Malacañang has confirmed that President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. is in good health. During a press briefing, Palace Press Officer Undersecretary Atty. Claire Castro addressed rumors suggesting ...

item.title: Ruth Odinga Urges ODM to Strengthen Its Presence Against UDA Expansion Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Ruth Odinga, the Kisumu Woman Representative, has openly criticised the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) for planning to field candidates nationwide, including in regions traditionally considered stro...

item.title: Russian VPN Restrictions Linked to Nationwide Banking Disruptions
item.description: Russia's recent efforts to restrict VPN use and clamp down on the messaging app Telegram have inadvertently caused major disruptions in the country's banking system. According to Telegram founder Pave...

item.title: Tanzania Revenue Authority Collects Sh9.31 Trillion in Third Quarter of 2025/26
item.description: Tanzania has recorded a strong tax collection of Sh9.31 trillion during the third quarter of the 2025/26 financial year, according to the Tanzania Revenue Authority (TRA). This performance shows stead...

item.title: Artemis mission makes historic lunar flyby after 54 years
item.description: The Artemis mission has entered a significant phase in its journey to the Moon, approaching a lunar flyby for the first time since 1972. The astronauts aboard the Orion capsule, including three Americ...

item.title: Why Kenya must tackle corruption to emulate Singapore's development path
item.description: The article argues that Kenya’s ambition to follow Singapore’s development success will remain out of reach unless the country deals decisively with graft. It uses Singapore as a reference point for e...

item.title: Eight Detainees Escape from Eastern Cape Police Cells, Authorities Launch Manhunt
item.description: Police in the Eastern Cape have launched a large-scale manhunt after eight men escaped from the Asfondering police station cells in Maluti. Details of how the escape occurred remain unclear, and inves...

item.title: Zanzibar continues tradition of commemorating former leaders through national prayer events
item.description: Zanzibar President Hussein Mwinyi joined wananchi, viongozi wa serikali, and family members in a special prayer session to honour the late former President Ali Hassan Mwinyi. The event took place at t...

item.title: US, Iran, and Regional Mediators Discuss Potential 45-Day Ceasefire in Ongoing Conflict
item.description: According to a report by Axios cited in multiple outlets, the United States, Iran, and a group of regional mediators are engaged in discussions over the terms of a potential 45-day ceasefire. The prop...

item.title: South Korea intelligence suggests Kim Jong Un's daughter could succeed him
item.description: South Korea’s National Intelligence Service (NIS) has indicated that it is reasonable to consider the teenage daughter of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un as his potential heir, signalling the highest ...

item.title: Johannesburg man drowns in Gonubie River during friends’ visit
item.description: A 30-year-old man from Johannesburg tragically drowned in the Gonubie River in East London on Sunday afternoon. The incident occurred during what was meant to be a casual outing with three friends, al...

item.title: RCMP tests AI-generated reports using body camera audio in Alberta and B.C.
item.description: The RCMP is experimenting with AI to streamline the process of drafting incident reports. The pilot project, running in Alberta and B.C., involves the use of Axon’s Draft One AI software, which conver...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Sharif expresses concern over Pakistani nationals injured in missile interception in UAE
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif expressed deep concern after three Pakistani nationals were injured in a missile interception at Khor Fakkan port in the UAE. The incident occurred during the ongoing con...

item.title: Zanzibar Religious Leaders Call for Peace and Unity During Easter
item.description: In Zanzibar, religious leaders dey encourage all Tanzanians to uphold peace, unity, and social harmony, pointing out say these values are key for national stability and development. Anglican Bishop Mi...

item.title: RBI considers repo rate amidst rising inflation risks due to Iran conflict and extreme weather
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is facing a complex decision regarding its repo rate as multiple global and domestic factors threaten inflation. The ongoing conflict in Iran has disrupted oil supplies...

item.title: Defence HQ Places Troops on Nationwide Alert for Easter with US Collaboration
item.description: The Defence Headquarters (DHQ) don put all troops across Nigeria on alert ahead of the Easter festivities to make sure say security tight. Maj Gen Michael Onoja, the Director for Defence Media Operati...

item.title: Medical experts caution on potential unintended consequences of bill against coerced sterilization
item.description: Canadian medical professionals have expressed concern over a new bill, Bill S-228, which aims to criminalize forced or coerced sterilization with penalties of up to 14 years in prison. While Indigenou...

item.title: Key factors behind Tanzania's enduring peace and stability
item.description: Tanzania has maintained long-term peace and stability thanks to the coordinated work of its security and defence organs under strong legal frameworks. Mr. Esaka Mugasa, an Assistant Lecturer at the Un...
